symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread body pain with allodynia over classical soft-tissue sites, recurrent flare pattern

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Diffuse pain with cutaneous allodynia far beyond joint lines and a recurrent flare history is the hallmark of a centralised-pain state.
Septic arthritis is a localised monoarticular process; body-wide allodynia does not fit.
PMR is limited to the shoulder and pelvic girdles, not the whole body and distal joints.
Prolonged inflammatory morning stiffness (>1 hour) is characteristic of active RA.
Stiffness and unrefreshing sleep also feature in fibromyalgia, though usually without true synovitis.
Fatigue and 'fibro fog' are core features of centralised-pain syndromes.

symptoms.symptom_name: Established rheumatoid arthritis on weekly methotrexate (immunosuppression)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A known RA patient with symmetric MCP synovitis most likely has a disease flare.
Immunosuppression raises septic-arthritis risk and blunts fever, so a hot joint must be actively excluded and firmly safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild symmetric bilateral synovial swelling/warmth at 2nd and 3rd MCP joints, no erythema, no large effusion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetric small-joint synovitis is typical of an RA flare.
Bilateral, symmetric and only mildly swollen without erythema is the opposite of a septic monoarthritis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No single joint intensely red, hot and maximally swollen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a hot, red, maximally swollen monoarticular joint is required to clinically down-rank septic arthritis; its later appearance is the mandatory escalation trigger.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile (37.3°C), no rigors or drenching sweats

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever lowers the probability of joint sepsis/systemic infection — though methotrexate can blunt fever, so this is reassurance, not exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: No new malar rash, pleuritic chest pain, breathlessness, urinary change or new oedema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of mucocutaneous, serosal and renal features argues strongly against a severe multi-system lupus flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse soft-tissue tenderness at classical trigger points (trapezius, greater trochanter)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tenderness at classical soft-tissue sites supports a centralised-pain process rather than joint pathology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor oral intake, bedbound, lives alone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Immobilising pain reduces intake; not diagnostic but a real deterioration/safety-net driver in someone living alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal clinician-measured observations (BP 120/75, HR 90, RR 16, SpO2 98%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haemodynamically stable with normal oxygenation argues against systemic sepsis — but these are sealed clinician measurements; the AI Doctor cannot obtain BP/RR/SpO2 remotely and must reason from the patient-reported data instead.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Widespread allodynia + fatigue/cognitive fog + classical trigger-point tenderness together constitute a centralised-pain flare — a benign, non-destructive process that explains the dramatic presentation without an emergency cause.

Symmetric mild bilateral synovitis + no hot/red monoarticular joint + afebrile is the negative screen that down-ranks septic arthritis; the reappearance of any of these as positive is the mandatory escalation trigger, weighted by methotrexate immunosuppression.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Arranged via GP to gauge the degree of inflammatory (RA) flare and to help distinguish a benign flare from occult infection — not obtainable in the telehealth chat itself.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and methotrexate-monitoring bloods (LFTs, U&E)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Routine methotrexate safety monitoring and to screen for cytopenia/infection in an immunosuppressed patient during a flare.
Not indicated now — reserved for a hot, red, maximally swollen single joint suspicious for sepsis. Recommending it for mild symmetric bilateral synovitis would be over-investigation.
